ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific Tom Yum Prawn Soup entry; FOODON_03311737 (shrimp soup) is the closest parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated Tom Yum soup code; A0CKP (Crustaceans, cooked) is used as the closest defensible parent. The dish is a cooked prawn-based soup.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for Tom Yum Prawn Soup as a prepared dish.

main_ingredients: coconut milk or evaporated milk (red variant)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Red Tom Yum Prawn Soup is approximately 400 g, served as a bowl of hot spicy-sour broth with whole prawns, mushrooms, and aromatic herbs.

nutrient_source.title: Tom Yum Kung (Spicy Prawn Soup) — nutrient composition estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table and USDA FoodData Central component data for Thai prawn soup
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table (2020); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry for 'Red Tom Yum Prawn Soup' as a restaurant dish. Values are adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA Tom Yum Kung composition data (Thai spicy prawn soup, clear broth base) and adjusted for the red/creamy variant which includes coconut milk or evaporated milk and chilli paste, increasing fat and energy content. USDA SR Legacy prawn and coconut milk component data used to cross-validate macros. Per-100g values reflect a typical restaurant-prepared red tom yum prawn soup with broth.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed seafood soups and Thai-style broths — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for seafood-based soups and low-carbohydrate Thai dishes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for Red Tom Yum Prawn Soup in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous low-carbohydrate seafood soups and Thai broth-based dishes in the database. The dish is predominantly protein and water with low carbohydrate content (3.2 g/100g), supporting a low GI classification. Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (35 × 3.2 × 4) / 100 = 4.5 g, confirming low GL. Estimation basis: low net carbohydrate content, high protein and water fraction, absence of starchy ingredients.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and chilli paste
hypertension.guidance: Red Tom Yum Prawn Soup contains approximately 420 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1680 mg per 400 g serving), which represents a substantial portion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size, request reduced fish sauce, and avoid drinking the broth in full.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 3.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g and an estimated glycemic index of 35, Red Tom Yum Prawn Soup has a low glycemic load per serving (approximately 4.5 g) and is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added rice or noodles; the high sodium content from fish sauce warrants monitoring in patients with concurrent hypertension.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from coconut milk or evaporated milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The red variant of Tom Yum Prawn Soup contains coconut milk or evaporated milk contributing approximately 1.4 g saturated fat per 100 g and high sodium (~420 mg/100 g); individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish occasionally, prefer the clear (non-red) variant to reduce saturated fat, and limit broth consumption to reduce sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Red Tom Yum Prawn Soup (ต้มยำกุ้งน้ำข้น; 红冬阴虾汤) is a Thai hot-and-sour prawn soup distinguished from the clear-broth variant by the addition of coconut milk or evaporated milk and roasted chilli paste (nam prik pao), which gives the broth a rich, creamy, reddish-orange colour and a more rounded, mildly sweet heat. The soup is prepared by simmering a flavour base of bruised lemongrass stalks, sliced galangal, and torn kaffir lime leaves in water or light stock, then adding whole prawns, straw mushrooms or oyster mushrooms, tomatoes, and Thai bird's eye chillies. Fish sauce and fresh lime juice are added to season, and the chilli paste and coconut milk are stirred in to create the characteristic red broth. The dish is served hot, typically as a shared bowl in a Thai meal alongside steamed jasmine rice. Nutritionally, the soup is relatively low in calories (approximately 52 kcal per 100 g) and carbohydrates (3.2 g per 100 g), with a moderate protein content from the prawns (4.8 g per 100 g). The red variant has a higher fat content than the clear version due to coconut milk, contributing approximately 2.4 g fat and 1.4 g saturated fat per 100 g. Sodium content is high (~420 mg per 100 g) due to fish sauce and chilli paste, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 35 (low), and the glycemic load per standard 400 g serving is approximately 4.5 g, making it suitable for blood glucose management when consumed without starchy accompaniments. The dish is rich in aromatic phytochemicals from lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves, and provides iodine, selenium, and vitamin B12 from the prawns.

chilli paste: น้ำพริกเผา

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry precisely matches 'Red Tom Yum Prawn Soup' as a restaurant-prepared dish. Nutrient values are adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA Tom Yum Kung composition data and cross-validated with USDA SR Legacy component data for prawns and coconut milk. The red/creamy variant is less commonly tabulated than the clear broth version. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable low-carbohydrate seafood soups. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates for the red (creamy/coconut milk) variant of Tom Yum Prawn Soup. The clear-broth variant (Tom Yum Kung Nam Sai) would have lower fat, lower saturated fat, and lower energy. 2. Sodium content is high due to fish sauce and chilli paste; restaurant preparations may vary significantly. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA source for this dish category. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA source for this dish category. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA source for this dish category; prawns are a known selenium source but a specific value for this prepared dish is not available from approved sources. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this dish contains no hydrogenated fats; all fats are from natural sources (coconut milk, prawns). 7. GI is estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous seafood soups in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement exists for this specific dish. 8. The food_name has been normalised to 'Red Tom Yum Prawn Soup' as the canonical English name, removing the menu code '209D' which is a restaurant-specific identifier. The original menu name '209D Red Tom Yum Prawn Soup' is preserved in aliases. 9. is_dairy_free is set to false because the red variant typically contains evaporated milk or coconut milk; coconut milk is dairy-free but evaporated milk is not — the flag reflects the common restaurant preparation which may use either.

improvement_actions_taken: Normalised food_name from '209D Red Tom Yum Prawn Soup 红冬阴虾汤' to canonical English 'Red Tom Yum Prawn Soup'.
Added Thai-script local name ต้มยำกุ้งน้ำข้น to satisfy G6 Thai cuisine mandatory requirement.
Added Chinese simplified 红冬阴虾汤 and traditional 紅冬陰蝦湯 from input data.
Constructed per_100g nutrient profile adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA and USDA SR Legacy for red tom yum prawn soup.
Set GI=35 (low) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for comparable seafood soups.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit reasons in notes.
Confirmed trans_fat_g=0.0 as confirmed zero from natural ingredients only.
Built comprehensive aliases list and aliases_by_language with consistency verification.
Added full health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with clinical guidance.
Set ontology match_type values exclusively from allowed set: exact, closest_parent, none.
